an4.40:1.0.1: Then the brahmin Ujjaya approached the Blessed One and exchanged greetings with him. When they had concluded their greetings and cordial talk, he sat down to one side and said to the Blessed One:
an4.40:1.0.2: (3) "But I praise a non-violent sacrifice at which cattle, goats, rams, chickens, and pigs are not slain, where various creatures are not slaughtered, that is, a regular giving, a sacrifice offered by family custom.(4) For what reason? Because arahants and those who have entered the path to arahantship attend a nonviolent sacrifice."
an4.40:1.0.3: When a sacrifice is timely and allowable,well prepared and nonviolent,the self-controlled followers of the spiritual lifeattend such a sacrifice as this.
an4.40:1.0.4: Those in the world who have removed the coverings,transcenders of time and destination,the Buddhas who are proficient in sacrifice,praise this kind of sacrifice.
an4.40:1.0.5: Having prepared an appropriate gift,whether of the ordinary kind or in memory of the dead,one makes the sacrifice with a confident mindto a fertile field, to followers of the spiritual life.
an4.40:1.0.6: When what has been properly obtainedis properly offered, properly sacrificed,to those worthy of offerings,the sacrifice is vast and the deities are pleased.
an4.40:1.0.7: The wise person endowed with faith,having sacrificed thus with a generous mind,is reborn in a happy world,ina realmwithout affliction.
